bundles.cold_call_definition: Elapsed time for the first bundle invocation. This may include Python setup inside the materializer, XLA compilation, first execution, internal host transfer/synchronization, and materializer-owned output writes. It is not pure compile or calculation time.
bundles.output_write_note: The benchmark separates standard certificate manifest/note writes after the cold materializer call.
bundles.warm_replay_note: Warm replay is disabled by default because it reruns every bundle and can roughly double benchmark cost. Pass --warm-replay to enable a second call with separate warm-replay scratch paths.

xla_compile_execution_split.reason: This harness measures the first materializer call as a cold call. XLA compile, first execution, internal host transfers, Python work, and materializer-owned writes are not separable at this boundary.

warm_replay.reason_if_disabled: Warm replay is disabled by default because it reruns every bundle and can roughly double benchmark cost. Pass --warm-replay to enable a second call with separate warm-replay scratch paths.

phase_definitions.bundle.cold_call_elapsed_s: First invocation of the bundle. This is the earliest honest boundary available here and may include XLA compile, first execution, internal sync/host transfer, Python work, and materializer-owned writes.
phase_definitions.bundle.cold_ready_block_s: Explicit block_until_ready on JAX leaves returned by the cold call. A zero value with no leaves means the bundle returned host summaries, so readiness already happened inside cold_call_elapsed_s.
phase_definitions.bundle.output_write_elapsed_s: Measured only when the benchmark can separate a benchmark-owned write from the materializer call; otherwise the bundle records that writes are included in cold_call_elapsed_s or not applicable.
phase_definitions.bundle.summary_elapsed_s: Small report-summary extraction after cold readiness and any separated benchmark-owned output write.
phase_definitions.bundle.warm_call_elapsed_s: Second invocation after the cold call, only when --warm-replay is enabled. This is a warm call boundary, not pure calculation time.
phase_definitions.process_startup_elapsed_s: Not measured from inside Python. This harness begins after the interpreter and module imports have already started.
phase_definitions.report_serialization_timing.elapsed_s: Serialization and writing of the benchmark timing JSON report.
phase_definitions.setup_timing.elapsed_s: Benchmark harness setup before bundles: output path creation, run resolution, bank slicing, and feedback-bin selection.

process_startup_elapsed_s.reason: Python code inside this benchmark cannot observe interpreter startup or module import time before the module begins executing.

report_serialization_timing.definition: JSON serialization plus writing the benchmark timing report.

setup_timing.definition: Path creation, run context resolution, perturbation-bank slicing, and feedback-bin selection before the first bundle starts.
